steps.action: Run goose down-to 0 to reset DB to clean state
Run goose up to apply all migrations
SHOW TABLES to verify expected tables exist
steps.observed: All 11 tables present: audit_logs, comment_actions, gitlab_discussions, gitlab_instances, hook_events, merge_requests, mr_versions, project_policies, projects, review_findings, review_runs, plus goose_db_version

steps.action: Capture table list and metadata after goose up (from VAL-FOUND-002)
steps.expected: 12 tables with InnoDB engine and utf8mb4_unicode_ci collation
steps.observed: 12 tables (11 app + goose_db_version), all InnoDB, all utf8mb4_unicode_ci (except goose_db_version)

steps.expected: All application tables dropped, only goose_db_version remains
steps.observed: OK 001_create_core_tables.sql (186.35ms), version: 0. SHOW TABLES shows only goose_db_version.

steps.expected: All tables recreated identically to before down
steps.observed: OK 001_create_core_tables.sql (303.6ms), version: 1. SHOW TABLES identical to pre-down snapshot.

steps.action: Compare table metadata before-down vs after-re-up
steps.expected: Identical table names, engines, and collations
steps.observed: All 12 tables match exactly: same names, same engines (InnoDB), same collations (utf8mb4_unicode_ci)

assertions.title: Database failure rolls back partial lifecycle writes

steps.action: Run TestTransactionalRollback: insert review_run in RunTx then return simulated error
Run TestTransactionalRollbackCommentActions: insert comment_action + audit_log in RunTx then return error
steps.expected: Transaction rolls back, 0 orphaned comment_actions and audit_logs

steps.action: Run TestPanicRollback: insert review_run then panic inside RunTx
steps.expected: Panic triggers rollback, 0 orphaned review_runs, panic re-raised
steps.observed: PASS — rollback triggered on panic, 0 orphaned rows, panic propagated

steps.action: Run TestRunTxRollbackPreservesOriginalError: simulate dual failure (fn error + rollback error via KILL CONNECTION)
steps.expected: errors.Join preserves original error matchable via errors.Is
steps.observed: PASS — original error preserved and matchable, enabling retryability

steps.action: Run TestRunTxRollbackErrorJoinSemantics: unit test for errors.Join behavior
steps.expected: Both original and rollback errors matchable via errors.Is

steps.action: Run TestTransactionalCommit: positive case - hook_event + review_run commit successfully

evidence.testResults: 6/6 tests PASS (TestTransactionalRollback, TestTransactionalCommit, TestTransactionalRollbackCommentActions, TestPanicRollback, TestRunTxRollbackPreservesOriginalError, TestRunTxRollbackErrorJoinSemantics)

frictions.description: No external fault-injection integration test exists that simulates MySQL crash mid-write from a running service. Current tests use simulated errors inside TxFunc and connection kills to validate rollback behavior, which is sufficient for the RunTx contract but does not exercise a full service-level recovery scenario.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed, 0 failed. VAL-FOUND-002: goose up creates all 11 expected tables cleanly. VAL-FOUND-003: goose down/up round-trip produces identical table structure. VAL-CROSS-014: RunTx correctly rolls back on error, panic, and dual-failure scenarios with no orphaned rows across hook_events, review_runs, review_findings, comment_actions, and audit_logs. Database left in good state (migrations applied).
